overview: Written in December 1947, just after the UN Partition Plan, this poem was inspired by a quote from Chaim Weizmann: "A state is not handed to a people on a silver platter." Alterman wrote it to honor the young men and women of the Palmach and Haganah who were fighting for independence. It depicts a nation waiting for a miracle, only to be met by a boy and a girl exhausted from battle who declare that they are the silver platter. It is the defining poem of Israel's Remembrance Day.
poem_lines: And the land grows still. The red eye of the sky
Slowly pales over smoking borders.
And a nation stands—heart-torn but breathing
To receive the miracle, the one and only.
It prepares for the rite, it stands in the crescent moon,
Wrapped in the festival’s fear and the light of the dawn.
Then from across, a girl and boy step forth,
And slowly march toward the nation.
Dressed in khaki and belts, heavy-shod,
On the path they climb in silence.
They have not changed their clothes, they have not yet wiped
The traces of the day’s work and the soot of the night.
Weary to the point of death, renouncing rest,
Dripping the dew of Hebrew youth.
Quietly they approach, and they stand still.
There is no sign whether they live or are shot.
Then the nation asks, tear-filled and wondering:
"Who are you?"—and the two are silent.
Then they answer her: "We are the silver platter
Upon which the Jewish state was given to you."
Thus they spoke, and fell at her feet in shadows.
And the rest shall be told in the chronicles of Israel.
poem_hebrew_lines: וְהָאָרֶץ תִּשְׁקֹט, עֵין שָׁמַיִם אֹדֶמֶת
תְּעַמְעֵם לְאִטָּהּ עַל גְּבוּלוֹת עֲשֵׁנִים.
וְאֻמָּה תַּעֲמֹד – קְרוּעַת לֵב אַךְ נוֹשֶׁמֶת
לְקַבֵּל אֶת הַנֵּס, הָאֶחָד, אֵין שֵׁנִי.
הִיא לַטֶּקֶס תִּכּוֹן, הִיא תָּקוּם מוּל סַהַר
עֲטוּפָה חֶרְדַת חָג וְאוֹרָהּ שֶׁל שַׁחֲרִית.
אָז מִנֶּגֶד יֵצְאוּ נַעֲרָה וָנַעַר
וְאַט אַט יִצְעֲדוּ הֵם אֶל מוּל הָאֻמָּה.
לוֹבְשֵׁי חָקִי וַחֲגוֹר, וְכִבְדֵי נַעֲלַיִם,
בַּנָּתִיב הֵם עוֹלִים, הֵם עוֹלִים בִּדְמָמָה.
לֹא הֶחְלִיפוּ בִּגְדָם, לֹא מָחוּ עוֹד בַּמַּיִם
אֶת עִקְבוֹת הַיּוֹם וְהַפִּיחַ שֶׁל הַלַּיְלָה.
עֲיֵפִים עַד בְּלִי קֵץ, נְזִירִים מִמַּרְגּוֹעַ,
וְנוֹטְפִים טַלְלֵי נְעוּרִים עִבְרִיִּים.
דּוּם הַשְּׁנַיִם יִגְּשׁוּ, וְעָמְדוּ לִבְלִי נוֹעַ.
וְאֵין אוֹת אִם חַיִּים הֵם אוֹ אִם יְרוּיִים.
אָז תִּשְׁאַל הָאֻמָּה, שְׁטוּפַת דֶּמַע וָפֶלֶא,
וְאָמְרָה: "מִי אַתֶּם?" – וְהַשְּׁנַיִם שׁוֹקְטִים.
יַעֲנוּ לָהּ: "אֲנַחְנוּ מַגַּשׁ הַכֶּסֶף,
שֶׁעָלָיו נִתְּנָה לָךְ מְדִינַת הַיְּהוּדִים."
כָּךְ יֹאמְרוּ וְנָפְלוּ לְרַגְלָהּ עֲטוּפֵי צֵל.
וְהַשְּׁאָר יְסֻפַּר בְּתוֹלְדוֹת יִשְׂרָאֵל.
